message:
fix(search): cap two-tier quality refinement to max_refinement_docs

Limit the number of fast-result candidates sent to the quality
(semantic/vector) scoring path by the configurable max_refinement_docs
threshold.  Previously, every fast result was forwarded for quality
re-scoring regardless of count, which could be expensive when the fast
tier returned hundreds of hits.

Key changes in TwoTierSearchIter:

- Extract candidates from only the first max_refinement_docs fast
  results instead of all of them, bounding the vector-similarity work.

- When fast_results exist but the candidate slice is empty (e.g.
  max_refinement_docs is 0), return the fast results as-is without
  attempting quality scoring.

- Build the blended-score vector with explicit capacity and index-based
  iteration so that results beyond the refinement cap retain their
  original fast score rather than being dropped entirely.  This ensures
  the final merged list still contains all fast hits, just with the
  tail portion unrefined.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
